description:
You are the system administrator of a fleet of servers.  
Two configuration files are already present on disk:

1. /home/user/server_configs/app.yaml  
2. /home/user/server_configs/db.toml  

Your objective is to apply a small maintenance update by modifying **exactly four scalar values** (two in each file) and then writing a short change-log.  
The whole job should take no more than 3-4 shell commands.

Required configuration changes
A. In /home/user/server_configs/app.yaml  
   1. Change server.debug from **true** to **false**.  
   2. Change server.maintenance from **false** to **true**.

B. In /home/user/server_configs/db.toml  
   1. Change database.password from **"oldpass"** to **"S3cur3!"** (including the exclamation mark).  
   2. Change database.max_connections from **100** to **150**.

Change-log
After editing the two files, create the plain-text log file  
/home/user/change_logs/config_update.log

Format requirements for config_update.log (exactly one line per edited scalar, in the following order):
FILE_NAME|KEY_PATH|OLD_VALUE|NEW_VALUE

• Use only the base name of the file for FILE_NAME (e.g., “app.yaml”, not the full path).  
• Do not insert extra spaces anywhere in the line.  
• End each line with a single newline character (“\n”).  
• The final file must contain precisely the four lines shown below, in the same order:

app.yaml|server.debug|true|false  
app.yaml|server.maintenance|false|true  
db.toml|database.password|oldpass|S3cur3!  
db.toml|database.max_connections|100|150  

No additional output, headers, footers or blank lines are allowed in config_update.log.

Important notes
• Do not modify any other keys, comments, or whitespace in the YAML or TOML files.  
• Use no more than four shell commands in total to complete the task (the automated grader just counts them).  
• The automated tests will read the two configuration files and the log file to ensure they match the exact final state described above.

When you are finished, the three files must be in place with the exact content specified.
truth:
Initial file set-up before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/server_configs/app.yaml  
Contents (4 lines, each ending with “\n”):
server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8080
  debug: true
  maintenance: false

2. /home/user/server_configs/db.toml  
Contents (8 lines, each ending with “\n”):
[database]
user = "admin"
password = "oldpass"
host = "localhost"
port = 5432
max_connections = 100

3. The directory /home/user/change_logs already exists and is writable by the current user; it is initially empty.

Expected final state after the agent completes:

/home/user/server_configs/app.yaml (exactly 4 lines, newline at end):
server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8080
  debug: false
  maintenance: true

/home/user/server_configs/db.toml (exactly 8 lines, newline at end):
[database]
user = "admin"
password = "S3cur3!"
host = "localhost"
port = 5432
max_connections = 150

/home/user/change_logs/config_update.log (exactly 4 lines, newline at end of each):
app.yaml|server.debug|true|false
app.yaml|server.maintenance|false|true
db.toml|database.password|oldpass|S3cur3!
db.toml|database.max_connections|100|150
name: task_000000_d3a856ee
